name: London Fire Brigade Incident Feed
contamination_reasoning: LFB incident data is published on the London Datastore but is not a standard benchmark dataset. The real-time feed (updated daily with same-day incidents) produces fresh records continuously. Unlikely to be in TSFM pretraining.
difficulty_note: Fire/EMS incident rates have strong diurnal and weekly patterns but also weather-dependent spikes (heat waves, storms), event-driven surges (New Year's Eve, Bonfire Night), and seasonal trends. Non-stationary with regime shifts during major events.
adapter_notes: Data available via London Datastore API (data.london.gov.uk/api). CSV downloads also available. Incident records include timestamp, incident type, borough, property type. Updated daily. No auth required for API.
verify: Confirm API endpoint structure and that incident timestamps are at minute-level granularity. Check update lag (same-day vs. next-day).

name: Kiva Microloan Funding Events
license: Public API (no key required for read endpoints)
contamination_reasoning: Kiva's API is public but the loan funding event stream as a time-series is not a known TSFM dataset. Real-time loan events are fresh by construction. The platform is well-known but its API data is unlikely to have been scraped for pretraining.
difficulty_note: Loan funding events have seasonal patterns (holiday lending spikes), geographic clustering, and viral effects (featured loans fund faster). Funding speed depends on loan amount, borrower country, and sector — a multi-factor forecasting problem.
adapter_notes: API at api.kivaws.org/v1/loans/newest.json returns newest loans with pagination. Poll every 1-5 min for new loan postings and funding completions. Rate limit: ~500 requests/hour. No auth required for read endpoints.
verify: Confirm API is active and returns loan posting/funding timestamps. Check pagination limits and rate limit headers.

name: OpenCollective Financial Transaction Events
contamination_reasoning: OpenCollective is a niche platform for open-source funding. Its transaction stream is obscure and unlikely to be in any pretraining corpus. Real-time transactions are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Transaction volume has project-lifecycle effects (fundraising campaigns, grant cycles), seasonal patterns (end-of-year giving), and bursty spikes from viral projects. Not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: GraphQL API at api.opencollective.com/v2/. Query for transactions with timestamps, amounts, and collective IDs. No auth required for public data. Poll every 5-15 min for new transactions.
verify: Confirm GraphQL endpoint is accessible and that transaction queries return timestamps at sufficient granularity. Check rate limits.

name: MalwareBazaar Threat Intelligence Feed
contamination_reasoning: MalwareBazaar is a security research platform. Its sample submission stream is obscure outside the cybersecurity community and unlikely to be in TSFM pretraining. Real-time submissions are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Malware submission rates have bursty patterns driven by active campaigns, zero-day releases, and research publication spikes. Not periodic — event-driven with clustering and regime shifts.
adapter_notes: API at bazaar.abuse.ch/api/ (POST requests). Query recent samples, get sample metadata including first_seen timestamp. Auth: API key for some endpoints, public for others. Rate limit: ~1000 requests/day for unauthenticated.
verify: Confirm API endpoints for querying recent samples and that first_seen timestamps are available. Check if API key is required for time-series-relevant queries.

name: NOAA National Hurricane Center Tropical Cyclone Advisories
contamination_reasoning: While NHC is a well-known public agency, the advisory RSS/GIS feeds are event-driven and produce fresh data every hurricane season. Historical storm tracks may appear in some datasets, but real-time advisory products (forecast cones, wind radii) are unlikely to be in TSFM pretraining as time-series. Post-cutoff storms are entirely unseen.
difficulty_note: Tropical cyclone intensity and track evolution involve nonlinear dynamics, sudden rapid intensification events, and regime shifts that a seasonal-naive baseline cannot capture. Storm-to-storm variability is high.
adapter_notes: RSS feeds at nhc.noaa.gov/index-at.xml and nhc.noaa.gov/gis/at.xml provide advisory summaries and GIS product links. Poll every 5-10 min during active storms. Parse advisory text for intensity (kt), pressure (mb), position (lat/lon). No auth required.
verify: Confirm RSS feed structure and that advisory fields (wind speed, pressure, position) are consistently parseable across storm types.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: Copernicus Emergency Management Service Rapid Mapping Activations
contamination_reasoning: Copernicus EMS is an EU program but its rapid mapping activation feed is obscure outside the geospatial community. Event-driven activations (floods, fires, earthquakes) produce fresh records continuously. Unlikely to appear in TSFM pretraining as a time-series.
difficulty_note: Disaster activation timing depends on unpredictable events (earthquakes, floods, fires). Activation frequency has seasonal patterns (e.g., European flood season) but also random spikes from sudden events. Spatial clustering and cascading activations add complexity.
adapter_notes: RSS/ATOM feed available at emergency.copernicus.eu/mapping/ems/rapid-mapping-activations/rss. Each activation has a type (flood, fire, earthquake), location, and series of product delivery timestamps. Poll hourly. No auth required.
verify: Confirm RSS feed is live and contains structured activation metadata (type, date, location code). Check if ec.europa.eu being in ALREADY_PROPOSED blocks this subdomain.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-05 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: HealthMap Global Disease Alert Feed
license: Free for non-commercial use (API key required)
contamination_reasoning: HealthMap aggregates disease outbreak alerts from news, ProMED, WHO, and other sources. While the underlying sources may be known, the aggregated alert stream as a time-series is unlikely to be in any TSFM pretraining corpus. Real-time alerts are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Disease alert frequency has seasonal patterns (flu season, dengue season) but also bursty spikes from unexpected outbreaks. Alert volume depends on media attention, reporting bias, and actual disease dynamics — a rich forecasting problem.
adapter_notes: API at healthmap.org/api requires free registration for API key. Returns alerts with date, location, disease, alert source. Rate limit: ~100 requests/hour. Poll every 15-30 min for new alerts.
verify: Confirm API key registration is still available and that the API returns structured alert data with timestamps. Check rate limits.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: OPTN Organ Transplant Registry Events
estimated_series_count: ~40,000 transplants per year across organ types
contamination_reasoning: Organ transplant registry data is published by HRSA but is not a standard time-series dataset in any known TSFM pretraining corpus. The data is updated periodically (monthly/quarterly), and post-cutoff records are unseen.
difficulty_note: Transplant rates depend on donor availability (seasonal, accident-driven), medical urgency distributions, and regulatory changes. Not trivially periodic — organ-specific trends have different dynamics (e.g., kidney waiting list growth vs. heart transplant urgency).
adapter_notes: Data available at optn.transplant.hrsa.gov/data/view-data-reports/. Downloadable as CSV/Excel. National and regional aggregates by organ type, transplant center, and time period. No API; scrape or bulk download. Update frequency: monthly.
verify: Confirm data download URLs are accessible and that monthly time-series can be constructed from the available tables. Check if data includes timestamps at sufficient granularity.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-05 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: Certificate Transparency Log Entries (crt.sh)
license: Public (CT logs are mandated by CA/Browser Forum)
contamination_reasoning: Certificate Transparency logs are public but are not a known TSFM pretraining source. The volume of new certificate issuances per time interval is a novel time-series. Real-time entries are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Certificate issuance volume has strong diurnal patterns (business hours), weekly seasonality, growth trends, and CA-specific behaviors (batch issuances). Also affected by domain expiration cycles and security incidents. Multi-seasonal with noise.
adapter_notes: JSON API at crt.sh/?output=json. Query by domain or list all recent entries. For volume time-series, count entries per time bucket. Poll every 5-15 min. No auth required. May be slow for large queries; use rate limiting.
verify: Confirm JSON endpoint returns entries with precise timestamps. Test query performance for recent entries.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-05 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: DE-CIX Internet Exchange Traffic Statistics
estimated_series_count: 1 aggregate traffic series per exchange point (~20 IXPs)
contamination_reasoning: DE-CIX is the world's largest IXP but its traffic statistics are not a known TSFM dataset. The data is published on their website, not in standard ML data repositories. Real-time traffic is fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Internet exchange traffic has strong multi-seasonal patterns (diurnal, weekly), growth trends, and event-driven spikes (major software updates, streaming events, outages). Distinguishes models that can handle multi-seasonal + trend + event components.
adapter_notes: Statistics page at decix.net/en/about/statistics/ may have an embedded JSON/API endpoint. Check for XHR calls in browser dev tools. Data includes throughput (Gbps) per 5-min interval. May need scraping if no API. No auth required.
verify: Inspect the statistics page for XHR/API calls that return JSON traffic data. Confirm data granularity (5-min vs. hourly) and historical availability.

name: CBP US Border Crossing Wait Times
contamination_reasoning: CBP border wait time data is public but obscure as a time-series. Not in any known TSFM pretraining corpus. Real-time wait times are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Border wait times have complex dependencies on time of day, day of week, holiday travel surges, staffing levels, and inspection policy changes. Multi-seasonal with regime shifts (holiday vs. normal periods). Not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: Data available at bwt.cbp.gov as XML/JSON. Updated every 30-60 min. Includes port, lane type, wait time (minutes), and update timestamp. No auth required. Poll every 30 min.
verify: Confirm the data feed URL (XML/JSON endpoint) and that wait times are published at 30-min granularity. Check for missing data gaps during off-hours.
_vet_reasons: already proposed on 2026-07-04 (status=proposed, seen 1x) — propose a genuinely new source

name: 511 NY Traffic Incident Feed
contamination_reasoning: 511 NY traffic incident data is public but not a known TSFM dataset. Real-time incidents are fresh by construction. The data is event-driven and unlikely to have been scraped for pretraining.
difficulty_note: Traffic incident frequency has diurnal/weekly patterns (rush hour peaks) but also weather-dependent spikes (snow, rain), event-driven surges (concerts, sports), and random accident clustering. Not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: API available via 511 NY developer portal. Requires registration for API key. Returns incidents with type, location, severity, timestamp. Poll every 1-5 min for new incidents. Rate limits apply.
verify: Confirm developer registration is available and API returns incident timestamps at minute granularity. Check rate limits and data update lag.

name: JAO European Cross-Border Capacity Auction Results
contamination_reasoning: JAO is the Joint Allocation Office for European electricity cross-border capacity. Its auction results are public but obscure outside the European energy trading community. Not in any known TSFM pretraining corpus. Auction results are fresh by construction.
difficulty_note: Auction clearing prices and allocated capacity depend on weather forecasts, generation mix, demand patterns, and grid topology changes. Multi-factor with seasonal patterns and event-driven spikes (outages, extreme weather). Not trivially periodic.
adapter_notes: Publication page at jao.eu/publications/auction-results. May have downloadable CSV/XML files or an API. Check for structured data endpoints. No auth required for public results. Poll hourly for new auction results.
verify: Confirm whether auction results are available via API or only as downloadable files. Check data granularity and whether timestamps are at auction-clearing level.
